content.problemStatement:
There are NNN chefs (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM dishes (numbered 111 through MMM). For each valid iii, the iii-th chef can cook dishes of exactly one type FiF_iFi​. Also, each chef is a friend of KKK different chefs.

Whenever some chef cooks a dish, he asks all chefs who either are his friends or know how to cook this dish to evaluate the dish. No chef may evaluate his own dish.

All the chefs are arranged in a row from chef 111 to chef NNN. Chefina is going to choose two valid integers LLL and RRR and allow the chefs L,L+1,…,RL, L+1, \ldots, RL,L+1,…,R to cook their dishes and have them evaluated as described above. She wants to choose LLL and RRR in such a way that each of the NNN chefs evaluates at least one dish. Find the number of such pairs.
constraints: 1≤Fi≤M1 \le F_i \le M1≤Fi​≤M for each valid iii
1≤Di≤N1 \le D_i \le N1≤Di​≤N for each valid iii
i !=Dii\space != D_ii !=Di​ for each valid iii
D1,D2,…,DKD_1, D_2, \ldots, D_KD1​,D2​,…,DK​ are pairwise disticnt
the sum of N⋅(K+1)N \cdot (K+1)N⋅(K+1) over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers F1,F2,…,FNF_1, F_2, \ldots, F_NF1​,F2​,…,FN​.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines contains KKK space-separated integers D1,D2,…,DKD_1, D_2, \ldots, D_KD1​,D2​,…,DK​ denoting the friends of the iii-th chef.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of pairs such that each chef evaluates at least one dish.
